emails.subject: Your AI Career Survival Checklist is here 📋
emails.preview_text: 27 actions — pick one to do today.

body.opening: Your AI Career Survival Checklist is attached — 27 actions to protect your income and get ahead in the AI era.
body.body_html:
Quick note before you dive in:

Most people download checklists, read them once, and do nothing. The 1-in-10 who actually act on it within the first 24 hours see the biggest results.

So here's what I want you to do right now:

  1. Open the checklist (it's attached or linked below)
  2. Find the one action that feels most relevant to your current situation
  3. Schedule 20 minutes tomorrow morning to do that one thing

That's it. One action. Tomorrow morning. That's the difference between a checklist and a result.
body.ps: P.S. — Tomorrow I'll send you the #1 mistake professionals make when they first try AI tools at work. It's not what you'd expect.

emails.subject: The #1 AI mistake professionals make (and how to avoid it)
emails.preview_text: It's not using the wrong tool. It's using it like a search engine.

body.opening: I promised you the most common AI mistake. Here it is:
body.body_html:
Using AI like Google.

Most people open ChatGPT or Claude and type vague questions — just like they'd search Google. Then they get generic, mediocre responses and conclude 'AI isn't that impressive.'

The problem isn't the tool. It's the approach.

AI responds to specificity. The more context, constraints, and clarity you give it — the better the output.

Compare these two prompts:

  • ❌ Weak: 'Write a marketing email'
  • ✅ Strong: 'You are a senior B2B email copywriter. Write a 200-word re-engagement email for SaaS customers who haven't logged in for 30 days. Tone: warm but direct. Include one specific value reminder (reporting feature). CTA: book a 15-min product walkthrough.'

Same tool. Completely different output.

The framework I use to write strong prompts every time is called RISEN. I'll share the full breakdown tomorrow.

emails.subject: The RISEN framework (copy-paste this)
emails.preview_text: 5 letters that will transform your AI outputs.

body.opening: Here's the RISEN framework I mentioned yesterday.
body.body_html:
Apply this structure to any AI prompt:

  • R — Role: 'You are a [specific role] with expertise in [domain]...'
  • I — Instructions: Tell it exactly what to do. Be specific. Include context about the audience and purpose.
  • S — Steps: Break complex tasks into numbered steps. AI follows sequences more reliably than open-ended instructions.
  • E — End goal: Define the output format. 'Produce a bullet-point summary', 'Write a 3-paragraph email', 'Create a comparison table'.
  • N — Narrow: Add constraints. Tone (professional/casual), length (max 200 words), what to avoid ('don't use jargon', 'avoid passive voice').

Practice this once and you'll never go back to vague prompts.

In The AI Blueprint, I've applied this framework to 15 pre-built prompts across 5 industries — so you can copy-paste them directly into your work. No framework needed once you have the library.
body.ps: P.S. — The guide is $37 with a 7-day money-back guarantee. If you don't find value, email me and I'll refund you immediately.

emails.subject: What your job looks like in 3 years (honest take)
emails.preview_text: Not doom. Not hype. Just the honest forecast.

body.opening: I want to share an honest perspective on AI and careers — not the version designed to scare you or reassure you.
body.body_html:
Here's what the research actually shows:

Tasks at highest risk: first-draft writing, basic research, data formatting, meeting summaries, templated communication, entry-level analysis.

Tasks most protected: strategic judgment, creative direction, complex stakeholder management, work requiring institutional knowledge and relationships.

The pattern: AI excels at producing competent averages. It struggles with original excellence that requires human judgment, expertise, and context.

Which means the professionals most at risk are those doing primarily average, repeatable cognitive work — and doing nothing to move up the value chain.

The 30-day plan in The AI Blueprint is specifically designed to help you audit your role, identify your vulnerable tasks, and create a concrete path to higher-value work — with AI doing the heavy lifting on the lower-value stuff.

Many of the professionals who've read it describe the same reaction: relief. Not because the situation isn't real — but because a clear plan makes it feel manageable.

emails.subject: Last nudge on this (promise)
emails.preview_text: I won't keep emailing about the Blueprint after this.

body.opening: I'm going to send one more email about The AI Blueprint, then move on — I promise.
body.body_html:
Here's what's inside:

  • 40+ pages of no-fluff AI career strategy
  • The RISEN prompt framework (plus 15 ready-made prompts)
  • 20+ industry scenarios across Tech, Marketing, Healthcare, Finance, Education
  • Salary data for 5 emerging AI roles
  • A week-by-week 30-day action plan

Price: $37. One-time. Lifetime access. 7-day refund if you're not happy.

If this isn't the right time, that's okay — the checklist you downloaded will still be useful.

But if you're serious about making AI work for your career rather than against it, this is the guide I'd start with.
body.ps: P.S. — After this email, I'll shift to sending useful AI career content every week — no hard selling. Stay subscribed for that if nothing else.

SEQ_02_BLUEPRINT_BUYER_ONBOARD.name: Blueprint Buyer Onboarding + Upsell

emails.subject: Welcome to The AI Blueprint 🎉 Here's what to do first
emails.preview_text: Start with Chapter 2, not Chapter 1. Here's why.

body.opening: Your AI Blueprint is ready. Thank you for trusting us with this.
body.body_html:
Before you dive in — a quick reading recommendation that most people ignore:

Start with Chapter 2, not Chapter 1.

Chapter 1 is the context-setting (useful, but not urgent). Chapter 2 is where the practical strategy starts — specifically, the role audit that will immediately tell you where your biggest opportunities and risks are.

Once you've done the audit in Chapter 2, go back to Chapter 1 and everything will make more sense.

Then: Chapter 3 (RISEN prompts) + Chapter 4 (your industry scenarios). That's the core.

I'll check in on Day 3 with a prompt exercise to get you started.
body.ps: P.S. — Reply to this email any time with questions. I read every reply.

emails.subject: Quick question about your AI Blueprint progress
emails.preview_text: Have you done the role audit yet?

body.opening: Hope you've had a chance to start The AI Blueprint. Quick question:
body.body_html:
Have you completed the role audit from Chapter 2?

It takes about 15 minutes and it's the single most clarifying thing most readers do. Once you have your audit done, the rest of the guide clicks into place.

If you haven't started yet — that's okay. Here's a shortcut:

Open a blank doc and write down every task you do in a typical week. Put an 'H' next to tasks AI could do, 'M' next to tasks AI could assist with, and 'L' next to tasks that require uniquely human judgment.

That's the core of the audit. The guide adds structure and industry-specific nuance on top of it.

Reply to this email and let me know: what's in your 'H' column?

emails.subject: How are you getting on with the Blueprint?
emails.preview_text: A small group of readers are going deeper — wanted to mention it.

body.opening: One week since you picked up The AI Blueprint — hope it's been useful.
body.body_html:
I wanted to let you know about something a small group of Blueprint readers are doing.

They decided they wanted to go beyond the strategy — to actually implement the system with:

  • 50+ advanced prompts specific to their role
  • Video walkthroughs of every concept in the Blueprint
  • Role-specific AI playbooks (6 industries)
  • An ROI calculator to track and present their AI gains
  • A 90-day implementation roadmap
  • A live 30-minute 1:1 call to build their personal AI strategy

That's The AI Blueprint: Pro Pack. It's $197 (one-time).

I'm not going to push this on you — the Blueprint guide is complete on its own. But if you're the type who learns by doing rather than just reading, the Pro Pack might be worth a look.
body.ps: P.S. — There's a 14-day money-back guarantee on the Pro Pack too.

emails.subject: Did you try the RISEN framework?
emails.preview_text: I'd love to hear how it's going.

body.opening: Two weeks in — how's the AI Blueprint treating you?
body.body_html:
Specifically: have you had a chance to try the RISEN prompt framework in Chapter 3?

Most readers report the biggest 'aha moment' comes when they first apply RISEN to a real work task. The difference in output quality is usually immediate.

If you want to share your experience — what you tried, what worked, what didn't — just reply to this email. I genuinely read these and they help me improve the guide.

Also: if you'd like a personal recommendation on how to apply the framework to your specific role or industry, the Pro Pack includes a 30-minute 1:1 call for exactly this purpose.

emails.subject: Before your strategy call — 3 questions to prepare
emails.preview_text: These will make our 30 minutes 3x more valuable.

body.opening: Your strategy call is coming up — here are 3 questions to think through before we talk.
body.body_html:
1. What's your role and what does a typical week look like? Be specific — the more detail you give, the more specific my recommendations can be.
  2. What's one result you want to be able to show your organisation in 90 days? Time saved? Output increased? New capability? Revenue tied to AI?
  3. What's the biggest barrier you're facing right now? Time to learn? Stakeholder buy-in? Knowing where to start? Team adoption?

You don't need perfect answers — these questions just help us use our 30 minutes well.

If you haven't booked yet: book here. Slots fill up, so don't wait.

emails.subject: Following up from your strategy call
emails.preview_text: Based on what we discussed, I wanted to mention something.

body.opening: I hope your strategy call was useful. Based on what typically comes up in those conversations, I wanted to mention something.
body.body_html:
Some Pro Pack clients — particularly those in leadership roles or with organisation-wide AI mandates — find that 30 minutes isn't enough to cover everything they need.

For those who want more depth, we offer extended AI strategy engagements:

  • AI Readiness Assessment — comprehensive audit of your team's AI opportunity and risk landscape ($1,500)
  • AI Implementation Roadmap — 90-day plan with tool selection, workflow redesign, and change management strategy ($4,500)
  • Ongoing AI Advisory — monthly strategy sessions + unlimited async support ($2,500/month)
  • Team AI Training Workshop — half-day session to upskill an entire team ($7,500–$15,000)

If any of these match what you're trying to accomplish, reply to this email with 'tell me more' and I'll send full details.

No pressure — the Pro Pack is complete on its own. This is just for those who want a deeper engagement.

email_structure.preview_text: One thing worth reading this week.
sections: 1 AI career insight or framework (200–300 words)
1 link to content (blog post, resource, or case study)
Optional: soft CTA to product or lead magnet

sample_issues.subject: The 5-minute AI audit you should do every Friday
sample_issues.preview_text: Track your time savings — or lose the argument for AI adoption.
sample_issues.hook: Most professionals feel AI is saving them time — but can't prove it.
sample_issues.insight: Spend 5 minutes every Friday logging: which tasks used AI this week, estimated time saved, output quality improvement (1–5 rating). After 4 weeks, you have data. Data is what you bring to management when pushing for AI tool budgets or making the case for a promotion.

sample_issues.subject: What separates an AI power user from an AI dabbler
sample_issues.preview_text: It's not the tool they use. It's the system.
sample_issues.hook: I've observed this consistently: power users have a prompt library. Dabblers start from scratch every time.
sample_issues.insight: Create a folder (Notion, Google Doc, anywhere) called 'Prompts That Work'. Every time you write a prompt that produces great output, save it. Name it by use case. After 30 days, you have a personal library that compounds. Your AI becomes faster every week — not slower.

If yes: click here to stay subscribed — I'll keep sending weekly insights on navigating the AI era.

If no: click here to unsubscribe — no hard feelings, genuinely.

If you're still interested but haven't had the chance to pick up The AI Blueprint yet, here's the link: lumenora.gumroad.com/l/hltwgb — $37, 7-day refund.
